question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly performance indicators for Store X in 2023, what can be concluded about the store’s overall performance across quarters?

options.A: Q1 has the best overall performance because it has one of the tallest stacked bars.
options.B: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines metrics with different units that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.C: Q3 clearly has the worst overall performance because it has the shortest stack in every metric.
options.D: Q4 has the best overall performance because it has the highest number of customer comments alone.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
